task_completion.description: The agent opened the Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot showing the main interface or search screen of the Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app.

task_completion.description: The agent searched for and viewed the interaction results between Vemurafenib and the Parkinson's medications.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The app screen displaying Vemurafenib alongside Ropinirole, Co-careldopa, and Entacapone, with traffic light color indicators for their interactions.

task_completion.description: The agent created the safety report file at the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal command (e.g., echo, cat, nano) or a text editor showing the creation or editing of a file explicitly named '/sdcard/parkinsons_safety_report.txt'.

task_completion.description: The safety report follows the exact required format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text report contains the exact keys: 'Cancer Drug: Vemurafenib', 'Ropinirole: [Color]', 'Co-careldopa: [Color]', 'Entacapone: [Color]', and 'High Risk Drug: [Name]'.

task_completion.description: The safety report assigns the correct interaction colors to the Parkinson's medications.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the report, Ropinirole is assigned 'Red' or 'Orange'. Co-careldopa and Entacapone are assigned 'Green', 'Yellow', or 'Grey'.

task_completion.description: The safety report correctly identifies the high-risk drug.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The report explicitly states 'High Risk Drug: Ropinirole'.

integrity.description: The agent actually used the Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app to find the interactions, rather than hallucinating the results.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the agent navigating the app and displaying the drug interactions before the text report is written.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the app by pasting a pre-written text block.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows a logical progression of opening the app, searching the specific drugs, viewing the results, and then writing the file.

integrity.description: The colors written in the final report match the colors shown in the app screenshots.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text colors in the report file correspond exactly to the traffic light colors displayed in the app for each drug pair.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Vemurafenib is the cancer drug. Ropinirole has a clinically significant interaction with Vemurafenib and must be classified as 'Red' or 'Orange'. Co-careldopa and Entacapone do not have direct interactions with Vemurafenib and must be classified as 'Green', 'Yellow', or 'Grey'. The 'High Risk Drug' must be identified as Ropinirole.
